Machine Shoulder Press.notes: Machine gives fixed path — great for beginners to learn pressing pattern safely.

Dumbbell Shoulder Press.notes: Requires more stabilisation than machine — greater range of motion.

Lateral Raises.notes: Pure lateral delt isolation. Keep a slight forward lean to load the middle delt optimally.

Face Pulls.notes: Extremely important for rotator cuff health and shoulder longevity. Do not skip.

Incline Dumbbell/Chest Machine.notes: The incline angle shifts emphasis to the upper / clavicular fibres of the pec.

Flat Chest Press Machine.notes: Horizontal press hits mid/lower pec fibres. Great for overall chest mass.

Dumbbell Bench Press.notes: Greater stretch vs barbell; dumbbells allow natural wrist rotation.

Pec Deck Fly (Machine/Cable).notes: Combined Pec Deck and Cable versions. Focus on pure pec isolation — no tricep involved.

Lat Pulldown.notes: Wide grip emphasises lat width. Lean slightly back for full lat contraction at the bottom.

Chest Supported Row (Seated).notes: Chest pad removes lower back fatigue completely. Focus on squeezing shoulder blades together at peak contraction.

Wide Grip Cable Row (Machine).notes: Wide grip cable version — focus on pulling the elbows back and wide for upper back detail.

Bicep Hammer Curls.notes: Neutral grip (hammer) hits brachialis (depth of arm) and forearms more than supinated curl.

Tricep Rope Pushdowns.notes: Rope allows wrists to flare out at bottom — better peak contraction than bar.

Over Head Tricep Extension DB.notes: Overhead position is the ONLY exercise that fully stretches the long head. High value.

Leg Press.notes: Foot placement changes emphasis: feet high = more glute/ham; feet low = more quad.

Goblet Squat.notes: Upright torso makes this very quad dominant. Holding weight in front forces core to work.

Smith Machine Squats.notes: Fixed bar path reduces stabiliser demand; allows focus on quad drive.

Seated Leg Extension.notes: Pure knee extension — only true quad isolation exercise. Important for VMO health.

Seated Hamstring Leg Curl.notes: Seated version keeps hip flexed — better stretch on hamstring short head. High value.

Cable Crunch.notes: Weighted ab work. Kneel and bring elbows to knees — DON'T use hip flexors to pull down.

Calf Raises.notes: Straight leg = gastrocnemius (outer calf). Bent knee version hits soleus deeper.

Seated Bicep Curl.notes: Seated position eliminates momentum — forces strict bicep contraction. Great for mind-muscle connection.

Preacher Hammer Curl.notes: Preacher pad locks the upper arm — eliminates cheating. Hammer grip shifts emphasis from bicep peak to brachialis depth.

Romanian Deadlift.notes: Hinge-dominant pattern. Eccentric hamstring stretch is the main stimulus — don't round the lower back.

Leg Extension.notes: Pure quad isolation. Emphasises VMO in the last 30 degrees of extension if pointed outward slightly.

Wide Stance Leg Press (Adductors/Hamstri.notes: Wide foot placement shifts load to adductors, glutes, and inner hamstrings — less quad-dominant than narrow stance.

Unilateral Lat Cable Row.notes: Single-arm cable row. Allows greater range of motion and helps correct left-right imbalances.
